'''Marcellin Wessel''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was one of the first regional governors, or [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]], of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Biography==
[[File:Threemoffs.jpg|thumb|left|Wessel (right), with fellow moffs [[Denn Wessex]] (left) and [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] (center), after being appointed in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].]]

Beginning as a [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]] commanding [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] forces on the more remote fronts of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Wessel was appointed by [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] under the [[Sector Governance Decree/Legends|Sector Governance Decree]], which authorized the creation of regional governors to administer entire [[Sector/Legends|sectors]]. Wessel's early rise to the administration of [[Immalia sector/Legends|Immalia sector]] hints at a history of unquestioned loyalty to Palpatine, since such appointments were made only on behalf of those who had proven their loyalty, and as moffs were intended from the start to be answerable only to the Emperor, it was crucial that they be beings who could be depended on without reserve. Shortly after the conclusion of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Moff Wessel, acting at the request of [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], submitted a [[Casualty Report: Order 66|report]] outlining the "highlights" of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine.

When [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine [[Declaration of a New Order|declared]] his [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Wessel was made [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] of the [[Immalia Oversector]] and assumed the role of commander of the [[Seventh Army|7th Sector Army]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

It is not known exactly what happened, but evidently Moff Wessel's fortunes turned for the worse. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], at the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], he had been relegated to the governorship of [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]], a dead-end position in a useless star system. Wessel decided that he could advance his career again by capturing Princess [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and kidnapped the leaders of the [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] [[Council of Elders (Yinchorri)|Elders Council]], offering to trade their lives back to the Yinchorri in exchange for Organa. With no choice, the Yinchorri agreed that their beloved elders were worth the life of one Human. They set a trap for Leia, but Wessel double-crossed the Yinchorri,<ref name="M86">[[Star Wars (1977) 86|''Star Wars'' (1977) 86]]</ref> angering them sufficiently to overthrow him and his regime. However, their insurrection was swiftly crushed by the Empire, ending with the killing of the Council and destruction of [[Tol-Kachorn]].

These events did not, however, stop the advancement of his son, [[Redd Wessel|Redd]], to becoming a [[General/Legends|general]] in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] and eventually head of [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Central Command]] under [[Carnor Jax]].

==Behind the scenes==
Governor Wessel was created in [[1983]] for the Marvel comics issue [[Star Wars (1977) 86|''The Alderaan Factor'']] by writer [[Randy Stradley]], currently in charge of the ''Star Wars'' comic lines for Dark Horse Comics. Like so many Imperial characters written in those days, he had no first name, and indeed it was not seen as relevant at the time. However, [[Daniel Wallace]], included the name as a sort of easter egg in his [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] article for [[Star Wars Insider 84|''Star Wars Insider'' 84]]. In his blog, he wrote: "'Meet Your Regional Governors' has profiles on [[Denn Wessex]] (an old WEG character from the gaming module [[Starfall (adventure)|''Starfall'']]) and Marcellin Wessel, who is intended to be the old man in charge of [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]] in the Marvel comic [[Star Wars (1977) 53|''The Last Gift From Alderaan'']] (one of the rare instances when Wallace may have been in error, as it was actually ''The Alderaan Factor''). General [[Redd Wessel]], who appears in ''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire]]'', would presumably be his son."<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|danwallace|25|HoloNet News: The Annotated Edition}}</ref>
